task_name: Builder live deploy anchor: Render URL and health proofs, no secrets
goal: Record the live Bluepilot Builder deploy as an anchor: Render service name, public URL, branch/root/build/start/health settings, the two live health proofs, and the set environment variable names only, with zero Neon identifiers or secrets.
scope_out: Do not write any Neon identifier (host/endpoint, project ID, branch ID, role, database name) or connection string anywhere in tracked files.
Do not write any secret value; environment variables are recorded by name only.
Do not change Builder code, tests, packages, or soulmatch.
Do not start the Maya-gate binding or the end-to-end probe in this task; those are later steps.
claims: docs/BUILDER_RENDER_DEPLOY_STATE.md records: Render service name bluepilot-builder, public URL https://bluepilot-builder.onrender.com, branch main, root directory builder, build command npm install --include=dev, start command npm start, health check path /health.
It records the two live proofs: GET /health returned HTTP 200 with status ok; GET /health/db returned HTTP 200 with status reachable.
It lists the set environment variable by name only: BLUEPILOT_BUILDER_DATABASE_URL (no value).
It contains no Neon host/endpoint, no project or branch ID, no role, no database name, and no connection string.
It notes the open follow-ups: free instance spins down on inactivity; the Neon spending-limit warning could matter under sustained use; Maya gate is still fail-closed until maya-core is reachable.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md are updated to reflect the live deploy.
assumptions: The live proofs were confirmed on 2026-05-31 (HTTP 200 ok and HTTP 200 reachable), independently verifiable by fetching the public URL.
The repo is public, so infra identifiers must stay out of tracked files.
dod: docs/BUILDER_RENDER_DEPLOY_STATE.md exists with the service name, public URL, branch/root/build/start/health settings, the two live proofs, and the env var name only.
A text scan of all files changed in this task finds no Neon endpoint pattern, no Neon project/branch ID, no role name, no database name, and no postgres connection string.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md reference the live deploy and the new anchor doc.

No Builder code, test, package, or soulmatch file is changed.
stop_conditions: Recording the state would require writing a Neon identifier, connection string, or any secret value.
A Builder code/test/package change becomes necessary.
soulmatch would need to be changed.
prior_task_findings: BP-132 added the runtime health entry; the service is now live on Render at https://bluepilot-builder.onrender.com with GET /health -> 200 ok and GET /health/db -> 200 reachable, confirmed both by Codex and by Claude fetching the public URL. BP-131 lesson: that doc task exposed Neon resource identifiers in a public repo; BP-133 must record the deploy with names and proofs only and zero infra identifiers or secrets. Next arc after this anchor is binding the canonical app-spanning Maya: deploy maya-core and set MAYA_CORE_URL plus the gate token so the BP-129 mayaBuilderGateClient (currently fail-closed) and the BP-123 memory client reach the real Maya.
